title: GSK Acquires 35Pharma for $950M, Anchoring Pulmonary Hypertension as Healthspan Bet
description: GSK's $950M deal for 35Pharma adds a clinical-stage pulmonary hypertension candidate (PAH and PH-HFpEF) to its pipeline and signals that big pharma now sees age-driven cardiopulmonary disease as a strategic healthspan category. Proof-of-concept trials are expected to start soon, and the size of the deal validates a real unmet need beyond legacy mechanisms.

title: Seragon's SRN-901 Extends Late-Life Mouse Lifespan 33%, Outperforming Rapamycin
description: Seragon Biosciences published preclinical data showing oral SRN-901, a five-component longevity stack, extended remaining lifespan in adult Western-diet-fed mice by 33% versus placebo while cutting frailty 70% and tumor incidence 30%. The intervention beat rapamycin head-to-head and is one of the strongest late-life intervention signals ever reported in a controlled mouse study.

title: Reprogrammed Cardiomyocytes Soften Heart Attack Damage - First Cardiac Reprogramming Win
description: A new Lifespan.io report (April 24) shows partial epigenetic reprogramming helps surviving heart muscle cells resume division after myocardial infarction in mice, easing damage. After Life Biosciences' eye trial, this is the strongest early hint that the same reprogramming toolkit could extend to cardiac repair, opening a much larger therapeutic market.

title: 'Energy Span' Reframes Fatigue as the Earliest Warning Sign of Aging
description: A new framework called Energy Span - covered in Longevity.Technology's Week 17 roundup - argues that mitochondrial decline manifests as fatigue years before chronic disease and can be tracked via wearables (HRV, glucose dynamics, sleep architecture). For practitioners, this gives early warning biomarkers and reframes fatigue as a measurable, actionable longevity endpoint, particularly for women.

title: Consumer Longevity Goes Mainstream: January AI on Medicare, OsteoBoost Funded
description: January AI joined Medicare's app library reaching 69M users with food-photo-based glucose prediction, while OsteoBoost raised $8M to scale a vibration wearable for osteoporosis (200M affected globally). Together these moves push longevity tools out of concierge clinics and into reimbursed mass-market care channels.

title: Reprogrammed Cardiomyocytes Soften the Blow in Heart Attack
description: Partial reprogramming helps heart muscle cells complete division after MI in mice, mitigating damage - opening cardiac applications for the same toolkit Life Biosciences is testing in human eyes.

title: GSK Acquires 35Pharma in $950M Deal, Advancing PH Drug
description: GSK acquires 35Pharma to add a clinical-stage candidate for PAH and PH-HFpEF, with proof-of-concept trials expected to begin soon.

title: Seragon Study Shows SRN-901 Extends Median Lifespan in Mice 33%
description: Oral five-component longevity drug extended remaining lifespan by 33% in adult Western-diet-fed mice, attenuated frailty by 70% and reduced tumor incidence by 30%, outperforming rapamycin head-to-head.

title: Energy Span Reframes Fatigue as Early Warning
description: New framework treats mitochondrial fatigue as a precursor to chronic disease that can be tracked through wearables (HRV, glucose dynamics, sleep architecture).

title: OsteoBoost Raises $8M to Scale Bone-Loading Wearable
description: OsteoBoost's vibration wearable mimics bone-loading to combat osteoporosis affecting 200M people, offering a non-drug alternative to bisphosphonates.

title: January AI Enters Medicare App Library, Reaches 69M Patients
description: AI-driven food-photo metabolic coaching joins Medicare's reimbursed app library, dropping the cost barrier for a 69M-strong senior population.

title: Epicutis TCP Serum Improves Skin Appearance 77% in 8-Week Trial
description: Topical TCP serum showed 77% improvement vs 56% in active comparator and zero improvement in placebo, marking a step forward in evidence-based skin longevity.

title: Tozorakimab Meets Primary Endpoint in Phase III MIRANDA COPD Trial
description: AstraZeneca's anti-IL-33 tozorakimab hit its primary endpoint in MIRANDA, supporting a key inflammaging pathway in age-related COPD.

title: AlzeCure Completes Phase 1B of ACD856 - Neurotrophic Brain Therapy
description: ACD856 enhances neurotrophic signaling to help brain cells communicate and survive better, with Phase 1B safe and well-tolerated.

title: Inbrain Completes Enrollment in First Human Graphene Brain Study
description: First-in-human graphene-based brain interface study fully enrolled - a step toward neurotech-enabled cognitive aging interventions.

title: FDA Clears First Cellular Rejuvenation Trial - $120B Longevity Market
description: Market commentary spotlights FDA clearance of the first cellular rejuvenation trial and a longevity market on track from $85B in 2025 to ~$120B by 2030.

title: How the Body Really Ages: 7 Million Cells Mapped Across 21 Organs
description: Rockefeller University team publishes in Science the most detailed atlas yet of aging across 21 mouse tissues, identifying cytokines as systemic intervention candidates.

title: Insilico Forms Industry's First Longevity Board Chaired by Lilly's Andrew Adams
description: Clinical-stage AI biotech Insilico Medicine launches a Longevity Board to oversee AI-driven aging research and dual-purpose drug discovery targeting hallmarks of aging.

title: The Immune System Ages Differently in Men and Women
description: Single-cell RNA study of ~1,000 people identifies sex-divergent immune aging patterns, calling for sex-stratified inflammaging biomarkers.

title: Rapamycin Might Blunt Exercise Response in Humans
description: New human data suggest chronic rapamycin can dampen exercise adaptations in older subjects, raising practical protocol questions for geroprotective dosing.

title: BioAge Reports Positive Phase 1 Data for BGE-102
description: BioAge's NLRP3-inhibitor program for metabolic diseases of aging shows positive Phase 1 tolerability and early activity, supporting later-stage development.

title: Life Biosciences Secures $80M Series D Financing
description: David Sinclair co-founded Life Bio closes $80M to complete Phase 1 ER-100 trial in optic neuropathies and extend its partial epigenetic reprogramming platform runway.

title: Inflammation's Statin Moment Edges Closer
description: Inflammation-modulating therapy may be approaching a regulatory and clinical turning point similar to statins for cholesterol - reshaping inflammaging treatment.

title: Mesoblast Invests in Smarter Cell Therapies with New CAR Deal
description: Mesoblast extends its mesenchymal precursor cell platform with a new CAR-cell deal - relevant for age-related musculoskeletal and inflammatory conditions.

title: Cells That Heal? New Parkinson's Trial Sparks Hope
description: A new cell-therapy trial in Parkinson's disease - a major age-driven neurodegenerative condition - opens additional translational paths for regenerative medicine.

title: Life Time Brings VO2 Max and Metabolic Testing to the Masses
description: Life Time fitness chain rolls out VO2 max and metabolic testing across its clubs, normalizing precision longevity diagnostics in mainstream gyms.

title: AB Science Links Masitinib to Survival Benefit in ALS Trial
description: Masitinib shows survival benefit in ALS, an age-related neurodegenerative condition, supporting tyrosine kinase inhibition as a translatable mechanism.

title: Mitrix Moves Mitochondria into the Clinic
description: Mitrix Bio reports Phase 1 safety with transplanted mitochondria in two older participants and opens Right-to-Try clinics in Newport Beach, Dallas, and Palm Beach.

title: Top Trends from A4M Longevity SpringFest 2026
description: Biological-age testing (GrimAge, DunedinPACE, OmicAge) enters routine clinical care; 'brainspan' emerges as longevity medicine's new cognitive endpoint.

title: David Sinclair Backs NAD+ Plus CD38 Inhibition Longevity Stack
description: Sinclair endorses a dual approach - boosting NAD+ precursors while inhibiting the NAD-consuming enzyme CD38 - as a next-gen geroprotective stack.

title: Bryan Johnson's Blueprint Protocol 2026 Update
description: 2026 refinements emphasize organ-specific metrics and frequent labs, reduce NMN/NR frequency, and add low-dose lithium and NDGA.

title: Updated Longevity Protocols for 2026 - Sinclair and Diamandis
description: Protocol notes from the Abundance Summit cover NMN 1g, resveratrol, nattokinase, spermidine, glycine, plant-based diet, pulsed fasting and 3-day fasts.

title: Precision Health Longevity Summit Opens in Calgary (Apr 25-26)
description: Two-day summit at Hyatt Regency Calgary brings practitioners and researchers together for breakthrough longevity insights and clinical applications.

title: Longevity Biotech Investment 2026: Set for a Breakout Year
description: DLT Chatbot estimates full-year 2026 longevity funding of $8-9B (55-60% above 2025's $5.72B), with Q1 at $3.74B across 49 deals.
